name: Vector3d
summary: The Vector3d class is used to represent vectors in a 3 dimensional space. Vectors in SketchUp have a direction and a length, but not a starting point. There are numerous tutorials on 3D vectors available on the internet.

constructors.name: initialize
constructors.summary: The new method is used to create a new vector.
constructors.remarks: The new method is used to create a new vector.

methods.name: linear_combination
methods.signature: linear_combination(weight1, vector1, weight2, vector2) => Geom::Vector3d | linear_combination(x, xaxis, y, yaxis, z, zaxis) => Geom::Vector3d
methods.summary: The Geom#linear_combination method is used to create a new vector as a linear combination of other vectors.
methods.remarks: The Geom#linear_combination method is used to create a new vector as a linear combination of other vectors. This method is generally used to get a vector at some percentage between two vectors. A linear combination is a standard term for vector math. It is defined as vector = weight1 * vector1 + weight2 * vector2

methods.summary: The #% method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #% method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors. This is an alias of the #dot method.

methods.summary: The #* method is used to compute the cross product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #* method is used to compute the cross product between two vectors. The cross product, also called the vector product, is an operation on two vectors. The cross product of two vectors produces a third vector which is perpendicular to the plane in which the first two lie. This is an alias of the #cross method.

methods.name: +
methods.summary: The #+ method is used to add a vector to this one.
methods.remarks: The #+ method is used to add a vector to this one.

methods.summary: The #- method is used to subtract a vector from this one.
methods.remarks: The #- method is used to subtract a vector from this one.

methods.name: <
methods.summary: The #< compare method is used to compare two vectors to determine if the left-hand vector is less than the right-hand vector.
methods.remarks: The #< compare method is used to compare two vectors to determine if the left-hand vector is less than the right-hand vector.

returns.doc: true if the vector1 is closer to origin than vector2

methods.name: angle_between
methods.summary: The #angle_between method is used to compute the angle (in radians) between this vector and another vector.
methods.remarks: The #angle_between method is used to compute the angle (in radians) between this vector and another vector.

methods.name: axes
methods.summary: The #axes method is used to compute an arbitrary set of axes with the given vector as the z-axis direction.
methods.remarks: The #axes method is used to compute an arbitrary set of axes with the given vector as the z-axis direction. Returns an Array of three vectors [xaxis, yaxis, zaxis] Vector3d objects

methods.name: clone
methods.summary: The #clone method is used to make a copy of a vector.
methods.remarks: The #clone method is used to make a copy of a vector.

returns.doc: a Vector3d object which is the clone of vector

methods.name: cross
methods.summary: The #cross method is used to compute the cross product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #cross method is used to compute the cross product between two vectors. The cross product, also called the vector product, is an operation on two vectors. The cross product of two vectors produces a third vector which is perpendicular to the plane in which the first two lie.

methods.name: dot
methods.summary: The #dot method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.
methods.remarks: The #dot method is used to compute the dot product between two vectors.

methods.name: inspect
methods.summary: The #inspect method is used to inspect the contents of a vector as a friendly string.
methods.remarks: The #inspect method is used to inspect the contents of a vector as a friendly string.

methods.name: normalize
methods.summary: The #normalize method is used to return a vector that is a unit vector of another.
methods.remarks: The #normalize method is used to return a vector that is a unit vector of another.

methods.name: normalize!
methods.summary: The #normalize! method is used to convert a vector into a unit vector, in place.
methods.remarks: The #normalize! method is used to convert a vector into a unit vector, in place. Another way to do this is vector.length = 1.0

methods.name: parallel?
methods.summary: The #parallel? method determines if two Geom::Vector3ds are parallel within a tolerance.
methods.remarks: The #parallel? method determines if two Geom::Vector3ds are parallel within a tolerance. Two vectors are parallel if there exists a scalar multiple between them.

methods.name: perpendicular?
methods.summary: The #perpendicular? method determines if two Geom::Vector3ds are perpendicular within a tolerance.
methods.remarks: The #perpendicular? method determines if two Geom::Vector3ds are perpendicular within a tolerance. Two vectors are considered perpendicular if their dot product is zero.

methods.name: reverse
methods.summary: The #reverse method is used to return a new vector that is the reverse of this vector, while leaving the original unchanged.
methods.remarks: The #reverse method is used to return a new vector that is the reverse of this vector, while leaving the original unchanged.

methods.name: reverse!
methods.summary: The #reverse! method is used to reverse the vector in place.
methods.remarks: The #reverse! method is used to reverse the vector in place.

methods.name: samedirection?
methods.summary: The #samedirection? method is used to determine if this vector is parallel to and in the same direction as another vector to within tolerance.
methods.remarks: The #samedirection? method is used to determine if this vector is parallel to and in the same direction as another vector to within tolerance.

methods.name: set!
methods.signature: set!(vector) => Geom::Vector3d | set!(x, y, z) => Geom::Vector3d | set!(array3d) => Geom::Vector3d
methods.summary: The #set! method is used to set the coordinates of the vector.
methods.remarks: The #set! method is used to set the coordinates of the vector.

methods.name: to_a
methods.summary: The #to_a method retrieves the coordinates of the vector in an Array[x, y, z].
methods.remarks: The #to_a method retrieves the coordinates of the vector in an Array[x, y, z].

returns.doc: the coordinates of the vector in an array

methods.name: to_s
methods.summary: The #to_s method is used to format the vector as a String.
methods.remarks: The #to_s method is used to format the vector as a String.

methods.name: transform
methods.summary: The #transform method applies a Transformation to a vector, returning a new vector.
methods.remarks: The #transform method applies a Transformation to a vector, returning a new vector. The original vector is unchanged by this method.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object to apply to the vector.

methods.name: transform!
methods.summary: The #transform! method applies a Transformation to a vector.
methods.remarks: The #transform! method applies a Transformation to a vector. The vector itself is modified.

params.name: transform
params.doc: A Transformation object to apply to the vector.

methods.name: unitvector?
methods.summary: The #unitvector? method is used to see if the vector is a unit vector.
methods.remarks: The #unitvector? method is used to see if the vector is a unit vector. This is equivalent to vector.length == 1.0

methods.name: valid?
methods.summary: The #valid? method is used to verify if a vector is valid.
methods.remarks: The #valid? method is used to verify if a vector is valid. A vector is valid if its length is not zero.

properties.summary: The #== method is used to determine if two vectors are equal to within tolerance.
properties.remarks: The #== method is used to determine if two vectors are equal to within tolerance.

properties.summary: The #[] method is used to access the coordinates of a vector as if it was an Array.
properties.remarks: The #[] method is used to access the coordinates of a vector as if it was an Array. The index must be 0, 1 or 2. The following are equivalent:

properties.name: length
properties.summary: The #length method is used to retrieve the length of the vector.
properties.remarks: The #length method is used to retrieve the length of the vector.

properties.name: x
properties.summary: The #x method is used to retrieve the x coordinate of the vector.
properties.remarks: The #x method is used to retrieve the x coordinate of the vector.

properties.name: y
properties.summary: The #y method is used to retrieve the y coordinate of the vector.
properties.remarks: The #y method is used to retrieve the y coordinate of the vector.

properties.name: z
properties.summary: Get the #z coordinate of the vector.
properties.remarks: Get the #z coordinate of the vector.
